description: Replace the existing multi-panel WebGL dashboard with a cinematic anime.js-driven scroll experience: large SVG F1 tire hero object, floating side callouts, GSAP ScrollTrigger chapter pin, Silverstone circuit background, scroll-velocity speedometer. Full spec at docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-16-dashboard-redesign-design.md. Full plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-16-dashboard-redesign.md. ALWAYS read those files before implementing each story.

userStories.title: Setup: GSAP install, circuitSvg.ts, CSS vars, isTechnicalMode
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the project infrastructure ready (GSAP installed, Silverstone SVG utils, new CSS vars, isTechnicalMode context state) so all animation components can be built.
acceptanceCriteria: Run: cd /Users/hussianaltufayli/Downloads/LatentLap-AI-main/dashboard && npm install gsap — no errors
File dashboard/lib/circuitSvg.ts exists with exports: CIRCUIT_POINTS (14 Point2D), buildCircuitPath(), CORNER_SVG (MB/Copse/Stowe/Club), lapToCircuitProgress()
dashboard/styles/globals.css :root has --compound-soft:#E8002D, --compound-medium:#FFC906, --compound-hard:#FFFFFF, --shap-pos:#FF8000, --shap-neg:#2979FF, --speedometer-size:120px
dashboard/styles/globals.css --sev-2 is #FF6D00 (was #FF4500)
dashboard/styles/globals.css has @keyframes glow-pulse-sev0 through glow-pulse-sev3 and pit-pulse
dashboard/styles/globals.css has @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block disabling all animations
dashboard/app/RaceContext.tsx RaceContextValue interface has isTechnicalMode: boolean and setIsTechnicalMode: (v: boolean) => void
RaceProvider provides isTechnicalMode state (useState(false)) and setIsTechnicalMode in useMemo value
All existing RaceContext fields preserved (currentLap, currentYear, currentDriver, etc.)
Typecheck passes: cd dashboard && npx tsc --noEmit

userStories.title: TireRings.tsx — 3 concentric SVG rings with independent anime.js rotation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the TireRings component so the tire hero has three independently rotating concentric rings driven by anime.js continuous loops.
acceptanceCriteria: File dashboard/app/components/TireRings.tsx exists with 'use client' directive
Component accepts prop severityColor: string and renders inside a parent SVG viewBox 0 0 400 400
Outer ring: circle cx=200 cy=200 r=170, strokeWidth=20, stroke=severityColor, clockwise 8s loop via animejs animate()
Middle ring: circle r=140, strokeWidth=12, stroke=severityColor opacity=0.5, counter-clockwise 14s loop with 6 spoke paths
Inner ring: circle r=110, strokeWidth=6, stroke=severityColor opacity=0.7, clockwise 22s loop with 8 tick marks
Center hub: circle r=80, fill=#0a0a0a — this is where severity number renders
Uses useReducedMotion hook from ../../lib/useReducedMotion — if true, skip all animate() calls
All animate() calls use animejs v4 API: import { animate } from 'animejs' — NOT anime({targets})
Animations use loop: true and ease: 'linear'
style={{ transformOrigin: '200px 200px' }} on each rotating <g>
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: TireTread.tsx — morphing SVG tread path via animejs morphTo
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TireTread to render and morph between 4 tread geometries (fresh→worn→slick) as severity changes using anime.js morphTo.
acceptanceCriteria: File dashboard/app/components/TireTread.tsx exists with 'use client' directive
Accepts prop severity: number (0-3)
Has TREAD_PATHS object with 4 SVG path strings keyed 0-3, all with same number of path commands so morphTo can interpolate
SVG <path> renders current severity path; on severity change morphs to new path via animejs: import { animate, morphTo } from 'animejs'
animate(pathRef.current, { d: morphTo(TREAD_PATHS[severity]), duration: 800, ease: 'inOutCubic' })
If reducedMotion: set d attribute directly without animation
Uses useReducedMotion from ../../lib/useReducedMotion
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: TireParticles.tsx — orbiting/ejecting severity-scaled particles
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TireParticles to render orbiting SVG circles around the inner ring, with count and speed scaling with severity (4 at sev0, 20 at sev3), and some particles ejecting at sev3.
acceptanceCriteria: File dashboard/app/components/TireParticles.tsx exists with 'use client' directive
Accepts props severity: number and severityColor: string
PARTICLE_CONFIG constant: { 0:{count:4,orbitR:90,speed:6000,eject:0}, 1:{count:8,orbitR:90,speed:4000,eject:0}, 2:{count:14,orbitR:92,speed:2800,eject:0}, 3:{count:20,orbitR:94,speed:1800,eject:4} }
Particles rendered as SVG <circle> elements inside a <g ref={groupRef}>
Orbiting particles animate using animejs keyframes array (60 frames of cx/cy positions around the orbit circle), loop:true
Ejecting particles (i < cfg.eject) at sev3 animate from orbit radius outward to 200px and fade opacity 1→0, loop:true
Uses import { animate } from 'animejs' — v4 API only
Uses useReducedMotion — if true, skip all animate() calls
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Speedometer.tsx — fixed top-left scroll-velocity gauge
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a fixed top-left F1 speedometer whose needle reacts to my scroll speed, so the experience feels dynamic.
acceptanceCriteria: File dashboard/app/components/Speedometer.tsx exists with 'use client' directive
Fixed position: top:24px left:24px, zIndex:60, 120x120px SVG gauge
Dark dial, arc track, tick marks at 0/100/200/300 km/h labels in Fira Code font
#FF8000 needle, center cap #FF8000
Listens to window scroll event (passive), computes |deltaY|/deltaTime as speedPxMs
Maps speedPxMs to needle rotation -135deg (0 speed) to +135deg (max speed = 4 px/ms)
Updates needle via animejs animate(needleRef.current, { rotate: deg, duration: 80, ease: 'outQuad' })
After 100ms of no scroll, decays needle back to -135deg via animate with outExpo ease, 1200ms duration
Speed label text updates in km/h (0-300 range)
Uses useReducedMotion — if true, skip animate calls
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SilverstoneCircuit.tsx — background SVG circuit with createDrawable and car
userStories.description: As a user, I want a faint Silverstone circuit SVG in the background that draws itself on load and has a small car following the track at the current lap position.
acceptanceCriteria: File dashboard/app/components/SilverstoneCircuit.tsx exists with 'use client' directive
Imports buildCircuitPath, lapToCircuitProgress from ../../lib/circuitSvg
Imports getAllLapsForDriver from ../../lib/data and useRaceContext from ../RaceContext
Full-size SVG (position:absolute inset:0 width:100% height:100%) with opacity:0.10 and pointer-events:none, viewBox='0 0 200 200'
Circuit <path> drawn via animejs createDrawable on mount: animate(createDrawable(circuitRef.current), { draw: ['0 0','0 1'], duration:2000, ease:'outCubic' })
Accepts props: activeChapter: number, topFeature?: string|null

Small <g> car (orange rect 6x10px) positioned using createMotionPath on the circuit path — or if createMotionPath API unavailable, use SVGPathElement.getPointAtLength(progress*totalLength) to position the car
Car position updates when currentLap changes
Corner glow circles visible only when activeChapter===2 (predictors chapter) — 4 circles at MB/Copse/Stowe/Club positions from CORNER_SVG
Uses useReducedMotion — if true, skip animate calls and position car statically
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CalloutLeft.tsx and CalloutRight.tsx — floating anime.js side text
userStories.description: As a user, I want data to float on the left and right of the tire as clean text that slides in with anime.js stagger when chapters change.
acceptanceCriteria: File dashboard/app/components/CalloutLeft.tsx exists with 'use client' directive
CalloutLeft accepts content: CalloutLeftContent (label, value, valueColor?, explanation) and visible: boolean
When visible becomes true: animate lines with translateX:[-40,0] opacity:[0,1] stagger(40) 360ms outQuart
When visible becomes false: animate lines out to right translateX:[0,40] opacity:[1,0] 240ms
File dashboard/app/components/CalloutRight.tsx exists with 'use client' directive
CalloutRight accepts content: CalloutRightContent (heading, rows?: ShapRow[], customLines?: string[], technicalDetail) and visible: boolean
ShapRow: { label: string; value: number; barPct: number }
CalloutRight renders SHAP rows with signed value labels (orange for positive, blue for negative) and progress bars
CalloutRight renders customLines as DM Sans text paragraphs
CalloutRight has + Technical / - Technical toggle button using isTechnicalMode from useRaceContext(), aria-expanded set correctly
When technical expanded, shows technicalDetail in Fira Code font
Both components use useReducedMotion and skip animations if true
Both export their content interface types (CalloutLeftContent, CalloutRightContent, ShapRow)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: LapScrubberFixed + ChapterDots + SettingsPopover — persistent nav UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want a fixed bottom lap scrubber, fixed right-side chapter dots, and a gear settings popover so I can navigate the experience.
acceptanceCriteria: File dashboard/app/components/LapScrubberFixed.tsx exists with 'use client' directive
Fixed bottom:0 left:0 right:0 zIndex:50, height:48px, rgba(8,8,8,0.95) backdrop-blur background
Shows LAP {currentLap} / {maxLap} in Fira Code, prev/next buttons, range input with accentColor #FF8000
Keyboard: ArrowLeft/Right change lap, registered via useEffect with cleanup
Uses getAllLapsForDriver(currentYear, currentDriver) from ../../lib/data
File dashboard/app/components/ChapterDots.tsx exists with 'use client' directive
Accepts activeChapter: number (0-4) and onSelect: (ch: number) => void
Fixed right:16px top:50% transform translateY(-50%), 5 dots, active=#FF8000 scaled 1.3x
Each dot has aria-label='Chapter N of 5: [name]'
File dashboard/app/components/SettingsPopover.tsx exists with 'use client' directive
Fixed top:20px right:20px, gear icon button (⚙), opens popover with year pills (2021-2025) and driver pills
Driver pills from getDriversForYear(currentYear) from ../../lib/data
Active year/driver pill shows #FF8000 background
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: TireHero.tsx — orchestrator: rings + tread + particles + scroll rotation + glow
userStories.description: As a user, I want the central F1 tire SVG to combine rotating rings, morphing tread, orbiting particles, a severity counter, and a glow layer — all driven by scroll progress and lap data.
acceptanceCriteria: File dashboard/app/components/TireHero.tsx exists with 'use client' directive
Imports and renders TireRings, TireTread, TireParticles as children inside an SVG viewBox='0 0 400 400'
Accepts scrollProgress: number (0-1), uses it to rotate whole SVG: deg = scrollProgress * 720
Reads current lap via getLap(currentYear, currentDriver, currentLap) from ../../lib/data
Reads getSeverityHex(severity) from ../../lib/severityColors for severityColor
Severity counter in SVG center: Rajdhani 900 font, 72px, counts up/down with animejs animate(counterRef, { value: severity, modifier: utils.round(0), ease: 'outExpo', duration: 600 })
Glow layer: absolute div behind SVG with radial gradient using severityColor, animation uses glow-pulse-sev{N} keyframe at correct duration (3s/2s/1.5s/1.2s for sev 0-3)
Compound color for ring stroke: read getLap().compound ('SOFT'/'MEDIUM'/'HARD'), map to CSS var (--compound-soft/medium/hard)
Pass compound color to TireRings as severityColor prop — NOT the severity glow color
Pass severity to TireTread and TireParticles; pass severity glow color to TireParticles as severityColor
Width/height: 50vmin container div
aria-label on SVG, aria-live='polite' on severity text element
Uses useReducedMotion — skip rotate and counter animation if true
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: ScrollStage.tsx — GSAP ScrollTrigger pin + 5 chapter content orchestration
userStories.description: As a user, I want a 500vh scroll stage that pins the tire and swaps chapter content (left/right callouts) as I scroll through 5 chapters.
acceptanceCriteria: File dashboard/app/components/ScrollStage.tsx exists with 'use client' directive
Imports gsap and ScrollTrigger: import gsap from 'gsap'; import { ScrollTrigger } from 'gsap/ScrollTrigger'
Registers: gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger)
Outer div ref={stageRef} style height:500vh; inner div ref={pinRef} position:relative height:100vh as the pinned viewport

ScrollTrigger.create({ trigger: stageRef.current, pin: pinRef.current, start:'top top', end:'+=400%', scrub:true, onUpdate: (self) => { setScrollProgress(self.progress); setActiveChapter(progressToChapter(self.progress)) } })
progressToChapter: returns 0-4 based on progress thresholds [0, 0.2, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8]
Renders ChapterDots with activeChapter and onSelect that scrolls to chapter
Renders SilverstoneCircuit, CalloutLeft, TireHero (with scrollProgress), CalloutRight as flex row centered in pinRef
chapterContent array: 5 entries, each with left: CalloutLeftContent and right: CalloutRightContent
Chapter 0 (Hero): left shows LatentLap-AI tagline/year, right shows what-is-this explanation
Chapter 1 (Severity): left shows severity label/number/explanation, right shows mode_probs breakdown
Chapter 2 (Predictors): left shows top predictor feature, right shows top3 SHAP rows from getSHAP()
Chapter 3 (Race Arc): left shows total laps/explanation, right shows current lap stats
Chapter 4 (Strategy): left shows pit strategy explanation, right shows recommendation description
SHAP rows built from getSHAP(currentYear, currentDriver, currentLap) — sorted by abs value, top 3
Keyboard PageDown/PageUp chapter navigation via useEffect
Typecheck passes

acceptanceCriteria: ScrollStage.tsx updated: when activeChapter===3, renders an absolute div of lap bands (one div per lap, flex-col, each colored by getSeverityHex(lap.severity_pred) at 25% opacity, pit stop boundaries shown as 1px #FF8000 bottom border)
When activeChapter===4, renders StrategyBars component beneath the tire

Bars within primary_pit_window have full opacity; outside have 50% opacity
StrategyBars positioned absolute bottom:60px, centered horizontally, 60% width

cd dashboard && npx tsc --noEmit shows 0 errors

Tire renders centered with rotating rings (outer/middle/inner at different speeds)
Severity number visible inside tire center
Speedometer fixed top-left
Gear icon fixed top-right
Lap scrubber fixed bottom
5 chapter dots on right edge

Scrolling causes tire to rotate and chapter callouts to update

userStories.notes: Build passes, tsc 0 errors. Browser QA: all UI elements render. Benign hydration warning for floating-point SVG path diff in TireRings (non-breaking). Fixed TireTread morphTo bug (pass CSS selector, not path string).

userStories.title: TireRings: createTimeline entrance + createDrawable stroke draw-in + clockwork spoke stagger
userStories.description: Inspired by animejs.com clockwork + line-drawing demos. Replace 3 independent animate() loops with a coordinated entrance via createTimeline(). Rings draw in via createDrawable() on mount (outer→middle→inner, stagger +300ms). Spoke paths stagger in with translateX, tick marks with translateY like the clock demo. Continuous rotation resumes after entrance. All disabled under reducedMotion.
acceptanceCriteria: On mount, outer ring strokes draw from '0 0' to '0 1' via createDrawable(), 900ms inOutExpo
Middle ring draws in 300ms after outer, inner ring 300ms after middle
Spoke paths stagger in: translateX [-4,0], opacity [0,1], stagger(40)
Tick paths stagger in: translateY [-6,0], stagger(20) (clockwork effect)
After entrance, rings resume constant rotation: outer 8s CW, middle 14s CCW, inner 22s CW
All entrance animations disabled when useReducedMotion() returns true
cd dashboard && npx tsc --noEmit passes (0 errors)
npm run build passes
No new browser console errors or warnings
userStories.notes: Implemented in dashboard/app/components/TireRings.tsx. Typecheck passes: cd dashboard && npx tsc --noEmit. Build passes: npm run build. createDrawable targets DOM SVGCircleElement refs, not selectors/groups; entrance uses createTimeline and cleanup pauses/reverts timeline plus pauses rotations.

userStories.title: TireTread: Replace CSS transition:d with animejs morphTo + createSpring ease
userStories.description: Inspired by animejs.com SVG shape morphing demo. Current code uses CSS transition: d 0.8s cubic-bezier — replace with animejs animate(pathRef, {d: morphTo('#tread-ref-N'), ease: createSpring({stiffness:180, damping:16})}). Hidden <defs> paths (#tread-ref-0 to #tread-ref-3) already exist in DOM from US-012. Remove the inline style transition.
acceptanceCriteria: CSS 'transition: d ...' style removed from the <path> element
On severity change, animejs animate() call with d: morphTo('#tread-ref-N') fires
Ease uses createSpring({stiffness: 180, damping: 16}) — not outExpo or cubic-bezier
morphTo argument is '#tread-ref-N' (CSS selector with #) NOT a raw SVG path string
Tread visibly morphs with spring bounce when switching between severity 0-3
reducedMotion: skip animate call, set d attribute directly via setAttribute
cd dashboard && npx tsc --noEmit passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in dashboard/app/components/TireTread.tsx. Uses spring({stiffness:180,damping:16}) (not deprecated createSpring). duration omitted so spring drives timing. morphTo('#tread-ref-N') resolves via document.querySelectorAll — hidden defs paths exist in same SVG. Code-review fix: removed duration:0 (was killing spring animation). tsc + build pass.

userStories.title: TireParticles: stagger() orbit + blend composition + spring appearance on count change
userStories.description: Inspired by animejs.com advanced staggering demo. Replace the manual 60-frame keyframe loop per particle with stagger() and function-based values. New particles entering (severity change increases count) spring into view with createSpring. Ejecting particles (sev 3) use composition:'blend' so they layer over orbit animations.
acceptanceCriteria: Orbit animation uses animate(particleEls, {...}) with function-based translateX/translateY rather than per-particle loops
delay: stagger(orbitSpeed / count) staggers each particle's start angle
On severity increase, new particles enter with scale:[0,1] opacity:[0,1] ease:createSpring({stiffness:280,damping:14}) delay:stagger(60)
Ejecting particles (sev===3) retain keyframe approach but add composition:'blend'
reducedMotion guard present — skip all animate calls
cd dashboard && npx tsc --noEmit passes
npm run build passes
userStories.notes: Implemented in dashboard/app/components/TireParticles.tsx. stagger spring entrance added (spring stiffness:280 damping:14, no duration). composition:'blend' on ejecting particles. 60-frame orbit keyframe loop kept (function-based translateX/Y approach was no-op — cos(angle+2π)===cos(angle)). Code-review fix: removed duration:0. tsc + build pass.

userStories.title: TireHero: spring counter + createAnimatable scroll rotation + entrance timeline
userStories.description: Inspired by animejs.com spring physics + timeline demos. Three improvements: (1) counter ease becomes createSpring({stiffness:300,damping:22}) replacing outExpo; (2) scroll rotation uses createAnimatable on svgRef for eased follow instead of linear scrollProgress*720; (3) mount entrance timeline sequences glow→rings→tread→particles→counter with staggered delays. Glow crossfades on severity change instead of instant color swap.
acceptanceCriteria: Counter animation ease is createSpring({stiffness:300, damping:22}) — no outExpo
SVG rotation uses createAnimatable(svgRef, {rotate:{duration:400,ease:'outExpo'}}) updated on scrollProgress change
Entrance timeline on mount: glow opacity 0→1 (400ms) → rings scale 0.85→1 (500ms outBack) → tread opacity 0→1 (300ms) → particles scale+opacity 0→1 (400ms outBack) → counter 0→severity (spring)
On severity change, glow div fades opacity 0→1 (crossfade, not instant swap)
reducedMotion: all entrance/spring skipped, instant initial values set
cd dashboard && npx tsc --noEmit passes
npm run build passes
No new browser console errors or warnings
userStories.notes: Implemented in dashboard/app/components/TireHero.tsx. spring() (not createSpring), createAnimatable scroll rotation, entrance timeline (glow+svg scale+counter), glow crossfade with race-condition fix (fadeOutRef/fadeInRef + latestGlowState ref). Code-review fixes applied: C1 race condition, I3 type casts, I4 timeline offset. tsc + build pass.
